description: Same content (same content_hash) submitted with a NEW Idempotency-Key. Registry MUST treat this as a fresh publish: HTTP 201 Created with a NEW ctx_id, even though the body bytes are byte-identical to a prior publish. RFC-ACDP-0003 §6.2 makes content-deterministic deduplication a producer-side optimization (§6.3); the registry only deduplicates on (agent_id, Idempotency-Key).

response_constraints.ctx_id: MUST be different from the ctx_id stored for (idem-key-AAAA, sha256:H1).
response_constraints.lineage_id: MUST be different — a new ctx_id deterministically produces a new lineage_id (RFC-ACDP-0001 §5.6).

expected.rationale: Idempotency is a per-(agent_id, key) deduplication mechanism. A producer that wants content-deterministic deduplication MUST track content_hash locally per RFC-ACDP-0003 §6.3 (the body's content_hash is the natural deduplication key); the registry does not do this on behalf of the producer because it cannot distinguish 'replay with new key' from 'intentional re-publish of the same content under a new context identity' (e.g., for testing, audit, or rotation).
